intermediate.title: Have to, must, should: Obligation, prohibition, necessity, advice
intermediate.content:
📌 Have to – Nghĩa vụ bên ngoài (External obligation)
Dùng khi nghĩa vụ đến từ quy tắc, luật lệ, hoặc người khác – không phải ý kiến cá nhân người nói:
You have to drive on the left in England. (= quy tắc giao thông)
We have to wear a uniform at school. (= nội quy trường)
I had to cancel the meeting. (= quá khứ, bắt buộc do hoàn cảnh)

⚠️ Have to KHÔNG phải modal verb – cần dùng do/does/did để tạo phủ định và câu hỏi:
❌ Have you to work tomorrow? → ✅ Do you have to work tomorrow?
❌ He hasn't to go. → ✅ He doesn't have to go.

✦ Have to dùng được ở tất cả thì:
I had to get up early. (quá khứ) / She's had to work weekends. (hiện tại hoàn thành)
✦ Trong văn nói thân mật, có thể dùng have got to / 've got to:
I can't stay; I've got to go to the supermarket.
📌 Must – Nghĩa vụ cá nhân (Personal obligation / opinion)
Dùng khi người nói tự cảm thấy cần làm việc gì, hoặc thể hiện ý kiến cá nhân:
I must go to the doctor. (= tôi tự thấy cần đi)
You must stop smoking. (= lời khuyên mạnh từ bác sĩ)

✦ Must chỉ có dạng hiện tại. Để nói về quá khứ, dùng had to:
❌ I musted go. → ✅ I had to go.

Must (chủ quan)Have to (khách quan)
You must buy a new suit. (ý kiến tôi)You have to wear a suit. (quy định công ty)
I must call her. (tôi tự nhắc mình)I have to call her. (ai đó yêu cầu tôi)
📌 Don't have to vs Mustn't – Hai nghĩa TRÁI NGƯỢC nhau!
Don't have toMustn't
= Không cần thiết (nhưng có thể làm nếu muốn)= Cấm! (bắt buộc KHÔNG được làm)
You don't have to wait here. (không cần, tự do)You mustn't wait here. (bị cấm đứng đây)
You don't have to come. (tuỳ bạn)You mustn't be late. (không được trễ)

✦ Cách nói khác của mustn't: can't / are not allowed to
You mustn't smoke here. = You can't smoke here. = You are not allowed to smoke here.
📌 Should – Lời khuyên / ý kiến đúng sai
Dùng để đưa ra lời khuyên hoặc nói điều gì là đúng/nên làm:
You should see a doctor. (lời khuyên)
You shouldn't eat so much sugar. (không nên)
He should apologise. (tôi nghĩ đó là điều đúng đắn)

✦ Should nhẹ hơn must: không phải bắt buộc, chỉ là gợi ý/khuyên nhủ.
You have to show your passport at the border. — You must remember to bring it!have to = quy định bên ngoài | must = nhắc nhở cá nhân
You don't have to finish the report today. — But you mustn't forget the deadline on Friday!don't have to ≠ mustn't

exercises.q: You ___ smoke in the hospital. It's strictly prohibited.
exercises.exp: Cấm tuyệt đối → mustn't

exercises.q: You ___ bring anything to the party – we have plenty of food.
exercises.exp: Không cần thiết nhưng có thể nếu muốn → don't have to

exercises.q: I ___ get up early tomorrow. My flight is at 6 a.m.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ do hoàn cảnh bên ngoài (chuyến bay) → have to

exercises.q: You look tired. You ___ take a few days off.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên → should

exercises.q: She ___ wear a uniform at her new school. It's the rule.
exercises.exp: Quy định nhà trường = nghĩa vụ bên ngoài → has to

exercises.q: ___ I have to bring a pen to the exam, or will they give us one?
exercises.exp: Have to là động từ thường → dùng Do/Does/Did để hỏi

exercises.q: Children ___ touch the exhibits in this museum.
exercises.exp: Bị cấm hoàn toàn → mustn't

exercises.q: The entrance is free, so you ___ pay anything.
exercises.exp: Không cần thiết (cổng vào miễn phí) → don't have to

exercises.q: I ___ remember to call Mum tonight – it's her birthday!
exercises.exp: Tự nhắc nhở bản thân → must

exercises.q: She was late, so she ___ run to catch the bus.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ trong quá khứ → had to (must không có dạng quá khứ)

exercises.q: You ___ tell anyone my secret. Promise?
exercises.exp: Cấm tiết lộ → mustn't

exercises.q: I think you ___ apologise to her. What you said was unkind.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên về điều đúng đắn → should

exercises.q: You ___ be able to speak French for this job – it's a requirement.
exercises.exp: Yêu cầu tuyển dụng = nghĩa vụ bên ngoài → have to

exercises.q: It's a casual party. You ___ dress formally.
exercises.exp: Không cần thiết → don't have to

exercises.q: You ___ use your mobile phone during the exam. It's against the rules.
exercises.exp: Quy định cấm → mustn't

exercises.q: You ___ eat more vegetables and less fast food. It's much better for you.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên về sức khoẻ → should

exercises.q: I've got to go now – I ___ pick up the kids from school in ten minutes.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ cụ thể do hoàn cảnh → have to

exercises.q: Passengers ___ keep their seatbelts on during take-off.
exercises.exp: Quy định hàng không, dạng thông báo → must

exercises.q: You ___ finish all the food on your plate. Take as much as you want.
exercises.exp: Không bắt buộc → don't have to

exercises.q: I ___ study more if I want to pass this exam.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên / điều cần làm → should

exercises.q: You ___ (must / negative) park your car here. It's a fire lane.
exercises.exp: Cấm đỗ xe → mustn't

exercises.q: She ___ (have to / past) work overtime last weekend to finish the report.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ trong quá khứ → had to

exercises.q: You ___ (not/have to) wear a tie. It's a casual office.
exercises.exp: Không bắt buộc → don't have to

exercises.q: I ___ (should) drink more water every day. The doctor told me.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên của bác sĩ → should

exercises.q: Students ___ (must) show their ID cards to enter the library.
exercises.exp: Quy định thư viện → must

exercises.q: You ___ (not/must) tell her the surprise – it's a secret!
exercises.exp: Cấm tiết lộ → mustn't

exercises.q: He ___ (have to / present) wear glasses to read since his surgery.
exercises.exp: Bắt buộc do điều kiện thể chất → has to

exercises.q: You ___ (should / negative) eat so much sugar. It's bad for your teeth.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên phủ định → shouldn't

exercises.q: We ___ (not/have to) leave yet. The train doesn't depart until 9.
exercises.exp: Không cần thiết vội vã → don't have to

exercises.q: I ___ (must) remember to book the tickets before they sell out.
exercises.exp: Tự nhắc nhở bản thân → must

exercises.q: You ___ (must/negative) run near the swimming pool. It's dangerous.
exercises.exp: Quy định an toàn → mustn't

exercises.q: She ___ (should) apologise for what she said. It was rude.
exercises.exp: Điều đúng đắn cần làm → should

exercises.q: Visitors ___ (have to) sign in at reception before going upstairs.
exercises.exp: Quy định khách đến → have to

exercises.q: You ___ (not/must) drive after drinking alcohol. It's illegal.
exercises.exp: Cấm theo luật pháp → mustn't

exercises.q: He ___ (should) take that job offer. It's a great opportunity.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên về cơ hội tốt → should

exercises.q: I ___ (have to / past) study very hard to get into university.
exercises.exp: Nghĩa vụ quá khứ → had to

exercises.q: You ___ (not/have to) buy me a gift. Just come to the party!
exercises.exp: Không cần thiết → don't have to

exercises.q: All employees ___ (must) attend the safety training next Monday.
exercises.exp: Thông báo bắt buộc từ công ty → must

exercises.q: You ___ (should / negative) stay up so late every night. You'll be exhausted.
exercises.exp: Lời khuyên không nên → shouldn't

exercises.q: The sign says you ___ (not/must) take photos inside the gallery.
exercises.exp: Biển báo cấm → mustn't

reorder.words: You mustn't park your car in front of the fire station
She had to work overtime to finish the project on time
You don't have to come if you don't want to
I must remember to call my parents this evening
Passengers must keep their seatbelts on during the flight
You should see a doctor if you feel that bad
He doesn't have to wear a uniform at his new office
Children mustn't touch the exhibits in the museum
We had to cancel the trip because of the bad weather
You shouldn't eat so much sugar every day
Do you have to work on Saturdays at your new job
She must apologise for what she said to him
You don't have to finish all the food on your plate
All students have to show their ID cards at the gate
I must study harder if I want to pass the exam
You mustn't use your phone during the driving test
The entrance is free so you don't have to pay
He should take that job offer because it is a great opportunity

You mustn't tell anyone about the surprise party

passage.text: When you start a new job, there are many things you {{1}} know and do. First, you {{2}} arrive on time every morning – it is a basic rule. You {{3}} be late, especially in the first few weeks, because it creates a very bad impression. You {{4}} also remember to introduce yourself to your new colleagues. However, you {{5}} memorise everyone's name on the first day – that would be too much! If you are unsure about something, you {{6}} ask your manager. You {{7}} try to do things incorrectly just to avoid looking foolish. After a few weeks, you {{8}} attend a formal training session. This is compulsory, so you {{9}} miss it. Finally, you {{10}} take long personal phone calls during work hours. If you follow these simple rules, you will {{11}} to settle in well. And remember – you {{12}} be perfect from day one. Everyone {{13}} learn, and your colleagues {{14}} be patient with you.

At our school, students {{1}} wear their school uniform every day. They {{2}} come to school in casual clothes – that is against the rules. However, students {{3}} buy a new uniform every year if the old one still fits. Phones {{4}} be kept on silent during lessons, and students {{5}} use them without the teacher's permission. Students {{6}} eat in the classrooms either. However, they {{7}} eat in the canteen or the outdoor area. After school, students {{8}} attend their clubs if they do not want to, but it is {{9}} recommended. Students {{10}} be respectful to all members of staff at all times. If a student {{11}} to miss a lesson, they {{12}} bring a note from their parents. Teachers {{13}} be consulted before any changes to the timetable are made. All students {{14}} take care of school property.

Looking after your health is very important. Doctors say that you {{1}} exercise regularly – at least three times a week. You {{2}} do an intense sport; even a gentle walk is beneficial. However, you {{3}} eat a balanced diet too. You {{4}} skip breakfast, as it gives you energy for the whole day. You {{5}} drink at least two litres of water every day. On the other hand, you {{6}} drink too much coffee or sugary drinks. You {{7}} visit your doctor once a year for a check-up, even if you feel fine. This way, your doctor {{8}} catch any problems early. You {{9}} wait until you are very ill to seek help. People often {{10}} take prescribed medicine exactly as directed. They {{11}} stop taking it early, even if they feel better. Children {{12}} eat chewing gum, and they {{13}} wash their hands before eating. Adults {{14}} lead by example.

When you travel abroad, there are many rules you {{1}} follow. First, you {{2}} carry your passport with you at all times in many countries. You {{3}} lose it, as replacing it abroad can be very complicated. You {{4}} also respect local customs and traditions. You {{5}} make fun of local food or traditions – it is rude and disrespectful. Before you go, you {{6}} check whether you need a visa. If you need one, you {{7}} apply for it well in advance. You {{8}} leave it until the last minute. At the airport, you {{9}} check in at least two hours before your flight. You {{10}} carry liquids over 100ml in your hand luggage. When you arrive, you {{11}} declare certain goods at customs. If you are unsure what to declare, you {{12}} ask the customs officer. You {{13}} worry about looking ignorant – they are there to help. And most importantly, you {{14}} enjoy every moment of your trip!

It was my first day working at the restaurant and I was very nervous. The manager told me that I {{1}} arrive fifteen minutes early every shift to prepare. I {{2}} be late under any circumstances, as it affected the whole team. During service, I {{3}} wear my uniform and keep it clean. I {{4}} use my phone while working – that was strictly forbidden. The manager said I {{5}} take a break during service, even if I was tired. However, after the rush was over, I {{6}} stay late if I did not want to. I {{7}} remember to greet every customer with a smile. I {{8}} overcharge customers or make mistakes with bills. After my first week, I {{9}} attend a food hygiene training session. It was compulsory. My supervisor told me I {{10}} pass the test at the end, but it was strongly recommended. She also said I {{11}} ask for help whenever I needed it. Overall, working there taught me that you {{12}} always stay professional, even when it {{13}} hard. And above all, I {{14}} never forget the customer comes first.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn vừa bắt đầu một công việc mới. Viết đoạn văn mô tả các quy định tại nơi làm việc mới của bạn – những gì bạn phải làm, không được làm, không cần làm và nên làm.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: have to, must, mustn't, don't have to, should
writing_prompts.example: I have just started working at a law firm, and there are many rules I have to follow. I must arrive before nine o'clock every morning, and I mustn't be late for client meetings. I have to wear formal clothes every day, but I don't have to wear a tie unless I'm attending court. I should always be polite on the phone, as the company has a very professional image. I mustn't share any confidential client information with anyone outside the office. Overall, the rules are strict but fair, and I think I should adapt quickly.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết lời khuyên cho một người bạn đang muốn học tiếng Anh giỏi hơn. Sử dụng các modal để phân biệt điều bắt buộc, điều nên làm và điều không cần thiết.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: should, shouldn't, have to, don't have to, must
writing_prompts.example: If you want to improve your English, you should practise a little every day rather than studying for hours once a week. You must be consistent – that is the most important thing. You don't have to spend a lot of money on courses; there are many free resources online. You shouldn't be afraid of making mistakes, because they are how we learn. You should also watch films and listen to English podcasts regularly. You mustn't expect results overnight, but if you stay motivated, you will definitely improve.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả các quy định tại một nơi công cộng mà bạn biết (bảo tàng, bệnh viện, thư viện, công viên…). Giải thích rõ điều gì bị cấm, điều gì bắt buộc và điều gì tuỳ ý.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: mustn't, have to, don't have to, should
writing_prompts.example: When you visit the National Museum, there are several rules you must follow. You have to buy a ticket at the entrance, but children under five don't have to pay. Once inside, you mustn't touch any of the exhibits, and you must not take photos with a flash. Visitors must keep their voices low so as not to disturb others. You don't have to join a guided tour, but it is strongly recommended because the guides give fascinating explanations. You mustn't bring food or drink into the galleries. Overall, a bit of respect for the space is all you really need.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang viết email gửi một người bạn đang chuẩn bị thi. Cho họ lời khuyên về những gì nên làm, không nên làm, và những gì họ phải chuẩn bị.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: should, shouldn't, must, have to, don't have to
writing_prompts.example: Hi Lan, I heard your big exam is coming up next week! Here is some advice. First, you should start reviewing your notes now – don't leave it until the last minute. You must manage your time carefully and make a study plan. You don't have to memorise every single detail, but you should understand the key concepts. You mustn't panic if you find something difficult; just skip it and come back later. The night before the exam, you should sleep well and eat a good breakfast. You mustn't stay up all night studying – it will just make you more tired. Good luck! I know you can do it.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết đoạn văn về các quy tắc an toàn khi tham gia giao thông ở thành phố. Phân biệt rõ giữa điều luật pháp cấm, điều bắt buộc theo luật và lời khuyên tốt.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, mustn't, have to, should, don't have to
writing_prompts.example: Road safety is extremely important, especially in a busy city. All drivers must have a valid driving licence and must not drive under the influence of alcohol or drugs – this is illegal. You have to wear a seatbelt at all times, and motorbike riders must wear a helmet. You mustn't use your phone while driving; even holding it is against the law. You should always check your mirrors before changing lanes and should signal before turning. Pedestrians must cross at designated crossings and mustn't jaywalk on busy roads. You don't have to rush – arriving a few minutes late is much better than causing an accident.

intermediate.title: Can, could, be able to: Ability and possibility
intermediate.content:
📌 Can / Can't – Khả năng và khả năng có thể
1. Khả năng hiện tại (present ability):
She can speak five languages. I can't swim very well.

2. Khả năng / xác suất xảy ra:
It can get very cold in winter here. This road can be dangerous at night.

3. Xin phép / cho phép (permission):
You can smoke on the terrace. Can I borrow your pen?

4. Yêu cầu ai đó làm gì (request):
Can you help me with this? (thân mật)
📌 Could – Khả năng quá khứ và yêu cầu lịch sự
1. Khả năng chung trong quá khứ (general past ability):
She could play the piano when she was six. He couldn't walk until he was 15 months old.

2. Yêu cầu lịch sự (polite request):
Could you pass the salt, please? (lịch sự hơn can)

⚠️ QUAN TRỌNG – Could vs Was/Were able to (quá khứ):
Khi nói về một lần thành công cụ thể trong quá khứ, dùng was/were able to (KHÔNG dùng could affirmative):
❌ I could escape from the burning building.
✅ I was able to escape from the burning building.
✅ I managed to escape from the burning building.

✦ Nhưng với phủ định, cả hai đều dùng được:
I couldn't / wasn't able to find my keys.
📌 Be able to – Dùng khi can/could không thể dùng
Can chỉ có dạng hiện tại và could (quá khứ). Với các thì khác, dùng be able to:

Tình huốngDùng be able to
Sau modal verb khácWill you be able to come? I might be able to help.
Sau to-infinitiveI'd like to be able to speak Japanese.
Present perfectI haven't been able to sleep well lately.
Sau -ingI like being able to work from home.

❌ I will can help you. → ✅ I will be able to help you.
❌ She has could finish it. → ✅ She has been able to finish it.
📌 Động từ giác quan + can
Các stative verbs (see, hear, smell, feel, taste) thường đi với can ở hiện tại và could ở quá khứ khi mang nghĩa giác quan:
I can smell something burning. She could hear the music from upstairs.
❌ I am hearing a noise. → ✅ I can hear a noise.
She could read before she started school, but she wasn't able to ride a bike until she was eight.could = khả năng chung | wasn't able to = thất bại ở lần cụ thể
I haven't been able to contact him since Monday. I hope I will be able to reach him tomorrow.be able to = dùng với thì hoàn thành và sau will

exercises.q: She ___ play the violin beautifully when she was only five years old.
exercises.exp: Khả năng chung trong quá khứ → could

exercises.q: I ___ speak French fluently by the time I finish my course.
exercises.opts: will be able to,can,could,was able to
exercises.exp: Sau will → be able to (không dùng will can)

exercises.q: After hours of trying, he ___ finally fix the engine.
exercises.exp: Thành công trong tình huống cụ thể → was able to (not could)

exercises.q: I ___ smell something burning in the kitchen.
exercises.exp: Stative verb smell → can (hiện tại)

exercises.q: ___ you pass me the salt, please?
exercises.opts: Could,Can,Are you able to,Would you can
exercises.exp: Yêu cầu lịch sự → Could

exercises.q: I'd really like ___ speak Japanese one day.
exercises.exp: Sau would like + to → to be able to (not to can)

exercises.q: I haven't ___ sleep well this week. I'm exhausted.
exercises.exp: Present perfect → haven't been able to

exercises.q: When I was young, I ___ run very fast, but now I get tired easily.
exercises.exp: Khả năng chung trong quá khứ → could

exercises.q: Even though the fire was spreading, the firefighters ___ rescue everyone safely.
exercises.exp: Thành công trong tình huống khó khăn cụ thể → were able to

exercises.q: I like ___ work from home. It saves me a lot of time.
exercises.opts: being able to,to be able to,can,could
exercises.exp: Sau like + -ing → being able to

exercises.q: This mountain road ___ be very icy in winter. Be careful.
exercises.exp: Khả năng/xác suất xảy ra → can

exercises.q: ___ I use your phone? Mine has run out of battery.
exercises.opts: Can,Could,Am I able to,Will I be able to
exercises.exp: Xin phép theo cách thân mật → Can

exercises.q: She ___ hear the neighbours arguing through the wall.
exercises.opts: could,managed to,was able to,has been able to
exercises.exp: Stative verb hear + quá khứ → could

exercises.q: We might ___ get tickets if we book early enough.
exercises.exp: Sau might → be able to

exercises.q: He ___ walk when he was just ten months old. He was very advanced.
exercises.exp: Khả năng chung ở trẻ nhỏ trong quá khứ → could

exercises.q: The hikers ___ reach the summit before the storm started.
exercises.exp: Thành công trong tình huống cụ thể trước khi bão đến → were able to

exercises.q: I ___ hear you. Can you speak up, please?
exercises.exp: Stative verb hear ở hiện tại → can't hear

exercises.q: Now that she's recovered, she ___ return to work next week.
exercises.opts: will be able to,can,could,was able to
exercises.exp: Tương lai → will be able to

exercises.q: He ___ speak three languages before he was ten.
exercises.exp: Khả năng chung ở trẻ nhỏ → could

exercises.q: It's great ___ travel freely between countries in the EU.
exercises.exp: Sau It's great + -ing → being able to

exercises.q: She ___ (can / past) run very fast when she was at school, but she's not so fit now.
exercises.exp: Khả năng chung quá khứ → could

exercises.q: I ___ (not / can / present perfect) get in touch with him all week.
exercises.exp: Present perfect → haven't been able to

exercises.q: Despite the heavy traffic, she ___ (manage / past specific) arrive on time.
exercises.exp: Thành công trong hoàn cảnh khó → was able to / managed to

exercises.q: I'd love ___ (speak / ability) Italian one day.
exercises.exp: Sau would love + to → to be able to

exercises.q: You ___ (can / request / polite) tell me how to get to the station?
exercises.exp: Yêu cầu lịch sự → Could you

exercises.q: I ___ (can / smell) something delicious from the kitchen.
exercises.exp: Stative verb smell → can smell

exercises.q: She ___ (not / can / past specific) finish the exam because she ran out of time.

intermediate.title: Modal verbs of deduction: Must, might, could, can't
intermediate.content:
📌 Deduction – Suy luận về hiện tại
Dùng modal verbs để suy luận khi chúng ta không chắc chắn 100% và dùng các bằng chứng để đưa ra phán đoán:

ModalNghĩaVí dụ
mustChắc chắn đúng (sure it's true)You must be tired after that long journey.
might / may / couldCó thể đúng (possible)She might be in the office. He could be sleeping.
can'tChắc chắn không đúng (sure it's NOT true)That can't be Tom – he's in Paris!

⚠️ QUAN TRỌNG: Khi suy luận phủ định = chắc chắn sai → dùng can't (KHÔNG dùng mustn't):
❌ He mustn't be hungry – he just ate. → ✅ He can't be hungry – he just ate.

✦ Can KHÔNG được dùng để suy luận:
❌ He can be at home now. → ✅ He might/could be at home now.
📌 Cấu trúc
modal + be + adjective/noun:
She must be a doctor. That can't be right. He might be busy.

modal + be + verb-ing (hành động đang xảy ra):
She must be working late tonight. They might be having dinner.

modal + verb (sự thật / trạng thái):
He must know the answer. She might live nearby.
📌 Must (deduction) vs Must (obligation)
ObligationDeduction
You must wear a seatbelt. (= bắt buộc)You must be cold. (= tôi chắc bạn lạnh)
She must finish the report. (= phải làm)She must be tired. (= chắc chắn mệt)
📌 Might, may, could – Sự khác biệt nhỏ
• might / may: có thể đúng – không chắc
• could: đây là một khả năng có thể – giống might
• might not / may not: có thể không đúng (nhưng không chắc)

She might not know about the party. (có thể cô ấy không biết)
It could be a mistake. (có thể là lỗi)

✦ Với phủ định CHẮC CHẮN → can't (không dùng might not / may not)
He knows every street in the city. He must live here. / That can't be right – the shop is closed on Sundays.must = chắc chắn đúng | can't = chắc chắn sai
She's not answering her phone. She might be in a meeting, or she could be sleeping.might / could = có khả năng, không chắc chắn

My manager came over and said: 'You {{11}} be wondering why we planned this secretly.' I nodded and laughed. It was wonderful. I {{12}} have asked for better colleagues. They {{13}} all be very kind people. I {{14}} have been happier working anywhere else.

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả một người lạ mặt mà bạn thấy trên đường và đưa ra các suy luận về họ – họ là ai, làm nghề gì, cảm thấy thế nào. Dùng must, might, could, can't.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must (sure), might/could (possible), can't (sure negative)
writing_prompts.example: I saw an interesting man at the bus stop this morning. He was wearing a white coat and carrying a stethoscope around his neck, so he must be a doctor or work in a hospital. He looked very tired and kept yawning, which means he must have finished a long night shift. He can't be a student – he looks at least forty years old. He was reading a thick medical textbook, which suggests he might be studying for a specialist exam. He could be a surgeon or a cardiologist. He was speaking quietly on his phone in a language I didn't recognise – he might be from another country, or he could simply be speaking a regional dialect. He looked serious but kind. He must be very dedicated to his work.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn nhìn thấy một căn nhà bỏ hoang trên đường. Viết đoạn văn suy luận về lịch sử của nó và những gì có thể đã xảy ra với nó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, might, could, can't for deduction about past and present
writing_prompts.example: I walked past an old abandoned house today. It must have been beautiful once – you can still see the elegant wooden carvings above the doorway. The garden is completely overgrown, so nobody can have lived there for many years. The windows are broken, which means the house must have been empty for a long time. Judging by the style of the building, it could have been built in the early twentieth century. Perhaps the family who lived there might have moved abroad, or something terrible might have happened to them during the war. There could be historical records about this place somewhere. It can't have always looked so sad. It must have been full of life and noise once, with children playing in that garden.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn nhận được một tin nhắn bí ẩn từ một người bạn không rõ. Suy luận xem người gửi là ai và tại sao họ nhắn tin cho bạn.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, might, could, can't for present and past deduction
writing_prompts.example: This morning I found a note in my locker that said: 'I know what you did last Friday.' I have no idea who left it. It can't be from my manager – she was at a conference all week. It might be from one of my colleagues who was at the office party on Friday. The handwriting looks familiar, so the person must know me quite well. It could be from my friend Peter – he was there and he loves playing jokes. Or it might be from someone who saw me accidentally spill coffee on the new printer and didn't say anything at the time. They must have decided to tease me about it anonymously. I'm not going to panic – it can't be anything too serious!

writing_prompts.vi: Suy luận về cuộc sống và tính cách của một người nổi tiếng (thật hoặc hư cấu) dựa trên những gì bạn biết về họ. KHÔNG cần dùng tên thật.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must, might, could, can't for deduction about character and lifestyle
writing_prompts.example: I've been thinking about what it must be like to be a world-famous musician. Fame must be exciting, but it can't always be easy. Having millions of fans who follow your every move must feel overwhelming at times. You might not be able to walk down the street without being recognised. The musician must work incredibly hard – recording, touring, and promoting music takes enormous energy. They might feel lonely at times, even surrounded by people, because it's difficult to know who your real friends are. They can't trust everyone they meet. However, performing in front of thousands of people must be one of the most thrilling experiences imaginable. It could be both the best and most challenging life at the same time.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn trở về nhà và thấy một số thứ trong nhà bị xáo trộn. Suy luận về những gì có thể đã xảy ra trong khi bạn vắng mặt.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: must have, might have, could have, can't have for past deduction
writing_prompts.example: When I came home this evening, I immediately noticed that something was different. The front door was unlocked, even though I always lock it. Someone must have come in while I was out. The kitchen window was open – I can't have left it like that because it was raining when I left. There were muddy footprints on the kitchen floor, which means someone must have come in from the garden. The biscuit tin was open and half empty, so whoever it was might have helped themselves to food. My cat was hiding under the sofa, looking very nervous. Something must have frightened her. Then I heard a noise from the living room. I cautiously opened the door and found my teenage nephew sitting on the sofa. He must have lost his key and climbed through the window. That would explain everything!

intermediate.title: Usually, used to, be used to, get used to
intermediate.content:
📌 Usually + Present Simple – Thói quen hiện tại
Dùng để nói về thói quen hoặc hành động lặp lại trong hiện tại:
I usually go to bed at eleven. She usually takes the bus to work.

✦ Usually KHÔNG có quá khứ dạng riêng – dùng used to cho quá khứ:
❌ I usually went there when I was young.
✅ I used to go there when I was young.
📌 Used to + infinitive – Thói quen/trạng thái quá khứ (đã chấm dứt)
Dùng để nói về điều đúng trong quá khứ nhưng không còn đúng nữa:

• Thói quen quá khứ:
I used to smoke. (= trước đây hút, bây giờ không)
She used to play tennis. (= trước đây chơi, bây giờ không)

• Trạng thái quá khứ (stative verbs):
He used to be very shy. (= trước đây nhút nhát, bây giờ không)
I used to live in Paris. (= từng sống, bây giờ không)

⚠️ Used to KHÔNG có dạng hiện tại! Dùng present simple + usually cho hiện tại:
❌ I use to go to the gym. → ✅ I usually go to the gym.

✦ Phủ định: didn't use to | Câu hỏi: Did you use to...?
I didn't use to like vegetables. / Did you use to live here?
📌 Be used to + noun / verb-ing – Đã quen với
Dùng để nói về điều gì đó mà bạn đã quen, không còn thấy khó khăn hay lạ lẫm nữa:

I am used to getting up early. (= tôi đã quen rồi, không còn khó nữa)
She is used to the cold weather. (= đã quen với lạnh)
He wasn't used to living alone at first. (= lúc đầu chưa quen)

✦ Be used to là trạng thái → dùng với simple tenses (không dùng continuous)
✦ Sau be used to: noun hoặc verb + -ing
I'm used to the noise. / I'm used to working long hours.
📌 Get used to + noun / verb-ing – Dần quen với (quá trình)
Dùng để nói về quá trình trở nên quen với điều gì đó mới hoặc khó khăn:

I'm getting used to waking up early. (= đang dần quen)
You'll get used to the food eventually. (= bạn sẽ quen)
She found it hard at first, but she got used to it. (= đã quen rồi sau một thời gian)

✦ Get used to = quá trình thay đổi (process) → có thể dùng continuous:
I'm slowly getting used to driving on the left.
📐 Bảng so sánh
Cấu trúcSau nó là gìNghĩa
used toinfinitive (bare)Thói quen/trạng thái quá khứ (không còn nữa)
be used tonoun / -ingĐã quen với (trạng thái hiện tại)
get used tonoun / -ingĐang/sẽ quen với (quá trình)
usuallypresent simple verbThói quen hiện tại
I used to hate vegetables, but now I am used to eating them every day.used to = quá khứ | be used to = đã quen (hiện tại)
She found it hard to get used to the cold, but after a winter she was used to it.get used to = quá trình | was used to = đã quen (kết quả)

exercises.q: It was hard at first, but I ___ waking up at five every morning eventually.
exercises.opts: got used to,used to,was used to,usually

writing_prompts.vi: Mô tả cuộc sống của bạn hồi còn nhỏ và so sánh với cuộc sống hiện tại. Sử dụng used to, didn't use to và usually để thể hiện sự khác biệt.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to (past habits/states), didn't use to, usually (present habits)
writing_prompts.example: When I was a child, I used to spend hours playing outside with the neighbours' children. We didn't use to have smartphones or tablets, so we made our own entertainment. I used to love riding my bicycle around the block and exploring the nearby fields. My mother used to cook every meal from scratch, and the whole family used to sit together at the dinner table every evening without exception. Life was simpler then. These days, I usually eat at my desk while working, and I rarely see my neighbours. I don't usually cook proper meals during the week because I'm too busy. I used to go to bed early as a child, but now I usually stay up until midnight. The pace of life is so much faster now.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một thay đổi lớn trong cuộc sống của bạn (chuyển nơi ở, bắt đầu công việc mới, học ở trường mới…) và mô tả quá trình bạn dần quen với hoàn cảnh mới. Dùng get used to và be used to.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: get used to (process of adapting), be/was used to (state of being accustomed)
writing_prompts.example: When I started my first job after university, I found the routine very hard to adjust to. I was used to waking up at nine during my student years, so getting up at six thirty every morning was a real shock. At first, I couldn't get used to the commute – spending ninety minutes on a crowded train twice a day was exhausting. I also wasn't used to sitting in meetings for hours and having to present my ideas formally. However, after about three months, I gradually got used to the schedule and started to enjoy the structure. Now I am completely used to office life and actually find it hard to stay productive when I work from home.

writing_prompts.vi: Hãy viết về thói quen và lối sống của một thành viên trong gia đình bạn khi còn trẻ, dựa trên những câu chuyện họ kể lại. So sánh với cuộc sống ngày nay.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to (past habits), didn't use to, usually (present), contrast
writing_prompts.example: My father grew up in the countryside and often tells me about his childhood. He used to wake up at dawn every day to help with the farm chores before school. He didn't use to have any toys or games – the children would make up their own games using sticks and stones. There used to be no television in his house, so the family would sit together in the evenings and tell stories. He used to walk four kilometres to school every day, regardless of the weather. He didn't use to eat in restaurants – that was something only wealthy families did. Life was physically harder then, but he says people used to be closer to their communities. Nowadays, we usually rely on technology for entertainment and communication, which is very different from how he grew up.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những thứ bạn đã quen với sau khi sống ở một nơi mới hoặc sau một thay đổi lớn trong cuộc sống, và những thứ bạn vẫn chưa quen hoặc bao giờ cũng thấy khó.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: am/was used to, get/got used to, still not used to, have never got used to
hint_words: I've never really got used to
writing_prompts.example: After living in the UK for three years, I am used to many things that once seemed very strange to me. I have completely got used to the British sense of humour – the dry jokes and understatement that confused me at first. I've also got used to queueing for everything, which is taken very seriously here! The weather no longer bothers me much; I'm used to grey skies and have learned to appreciate the rare sunny days. However, I'm still not used to how reserved people are on public transport – nobody makes eye contact or speaks to strangers. I've also never really got used to eating dinner at five in the afternoon. In my culture, dinner is at eight or nine o'clock, so eating so early still feels odd. But overall, I'm slowly getting used to life here, one small cultural difference at a time.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về sự thay đổi trong xã hội hoặc công nghệ – những gì người ta đã quen làm ngày xưa, những gì người ta đã quen với ngày nay, và dự đoán những gì người ta sẽ quen trong tương lai.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: used to (past), are used to (present), will get used to (future)
hint_words: in the future people will get used to
writing_prompts.example: Just thirty years ago, people used to rely entirely on physical maps when travelling. Nobody was used to having turn-by-turn navigation in their pocket. People used to visit travel agents to book holidays and used to wait weeks for photographs to be developed. Today, we are completely used to instant access to information, online booking, and digital photography. We have also got used to shopping online and receiving deliveries the next day – something that would have seemed extraordinary in the 1990s. Looking ahead, I believe future generations will quickly get used to using artificial intelligence assistants for everyday tasks, from managing their schedules to giving medical advice. They will probably get used to self-driving cars and cashless societies. Technology that seems futuristic today will simply be normal for them. It is remarkable how quickly humans get used to new ways of living.

intermediate.title: Had better… It's time…
intermediate.content:
📌 Had better – Lời khuyên khẩn cấp / Cảnh báo (Urgent advice / Warning)
Dùng để đưa ra lời khuyên mạnh hoặc cảnh báo – ngụ ý sẽ có hậu quả xấu nếu không làm theo:
I'd better go now or I'll miss the bus.
You'd better see a doctor. That cough sounds serious.
He'd better give me my money back, or he'll be in trouble.

✦ Cấu trúc: had better + infinitive (không "to")
❌ You'd better to go home. → ✅ You'd better go home.

✦ Thể phủ định: had better not + infinitive
You'd better not say a word about this.
I'd better not forget her birthday again.

✦ Trong văn nói thường dùng dạng viết tắt ''d better'. Đôi khi người ta chỉ nói 'better' (bỏ had):
You better go home. I better get it right this time.

⚠️ Had là dạng quá khứ nhưng had better CHỈ dùng cho hiện tại / tương lai gần:
You'd better call me soon. (= tương lai gần)
📌 Had better vs Should
Should – lời khuyên chung, không có hậu quả cụ thể nếu không làm:
You should try the cake. It's delicious. (gợi ý bình thường)

Had better – lời khuyên khẩn cấp, có hậu quả nếu không làm:
You'd better hurry up or you'll miss the bus. (cảnh báo)

✦ Should dùng trong tình huống chung chung; had better chỉ dùng cho tình huống cụ thể trước mắt:
❌ You'd better exercise more often. (tình huống chung → dùng should)
✅ You should exercise more often.
📌 It's time – Đã đến lúc phải làm gì đó
Cấu trúc 1: It's time + to + infinitive – nói về điều nên làm ngay bây giờ hoặc tương lai:
It's time to go home.
It's time for you to make a decision.

Cấu trúc 2: It's (about/high) time + subject + past simple – nói rằng ai đó nên làm điều gì đó ngay bây giờ (hoặc đã nên làm rồi). Dùng quá khứ đơn nhưng mang nghĩa hiện tại/tương lai:
It's time you went to the doctor. (= You should go now)
It's about time you stopped smoking.
It's high time he found a proper job.

⚠️ Lưu ý: "About time" và "high time" nhấn mạnh hơn – ngụ ý việc đó đáng lẽ phải xảy ra sớm hơn rồi.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn là người hướng dẫn một người bạn mới đến thành phố của bạn. Họ đang phạm nhiều sai lầm. Hãy viết đoạn văn đưa ra các lời khuyên khẩn cấp, sử dụng 'had better' và 'it's time', mô tả ít nhất 5 tình huống cụ thể.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: had better, had better not, it's time + past simple, it's about/high time
writing_prompts.example: My new friend Jake has just moved to Ho Chi Minh City and he is already making some serious mistakes. First, he'd better not drink tap water here — it is not safe, and he'll get sick if he does. It's time he bought a water filter or started drinking bottled water. He'd better also learn a few basic Vietnamese phrases, or people will find it very difficult to help him. It's about time he got a local SIM card too, because international roaming costs a fortune. He'd better not leave his valuables on the table at cafés, or someone might take them. It is high time he found a good local market to buy fresh food, because eating at tourist restaurants every day is very expensive. I'd better take him around the city this weekend so he can learn the basics quickly.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về tình trạng môi trường hiện nay và những gì mọi người, doanh nghiệp và chính phủ cần làm ngay bây giờ. Sử dụng 'had better', 'it's time', 'it's high time' để nhấn mạnh sự khẩn cấp.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: had better, it's high time, it's about time + past simple, or else
writing_prompts.example: The state of our environment is alarming, and it is high time we took serious action. Governments had better stop making empty promises and start introducing meaningful policies to reduce carbon emissions immediately. It is about time they banned the use of single-use plastics completely. Companies had better invest in renewable energy sources or face heavy financial penalties. Individual citizens had better also change their daily habits — we should drive less, recycle more, and reduce energy consumption at home. It is high time schools educated young people about environmental responsibility from an early age. The planet had better not be pushed any further towards disaster, or the consequences for future generations will be truly devastating.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một người bạn hoặc thành viên gia đình đang có những thói quen không tốt. Đưa ra lời khuyên và cảnh báo, sử dụng cả 'had better' lẫn 'it's time/it's high time'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: had better, had better not, it's time + to infinitive, it's high time + past simple
writing_prompts.example: My brother has developed some very unhealthy habits over the past year, and I am genuinely worried about him. He had better stop staying up until three in the morning every night, because sleep deprivation is seriously affecting his work performance. He'd better not skip breakfast either — it's the most important meal of the day. It's time he started exercising regularly, even if it's just a thirty-minute walk each day. He'd better cut down on junk food before his health gets any worse. It is high time he spoke to a doctor about his constant tiredness. He had better also stop spending so much money on unnecessary things online, or he will end up in debt. It's about time he took his wellbeing seriously, because no one can do it for him.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn là giám đốc của một công ty đang gặp khó khăn. Viết bài phát biểu ngắn cho nhân viên, đưa ra các yêu cầu và lời cảnh báo khẩn cấp sử dụng 'had better' và 'it's time'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: had better (positive and negative), it's time + to infinitive, it's high time + past simple
writing_prompts.example: Good morning, everyone. I want to address some serious concerns about the direction of our company. Sales have been falling for three consecutive months, and it is high time we acknowledged that our current strategy is not working. We had better change our approach immediately, or we will lose even more customers. Our customer service team had better start responding to complaints within twenty-four hours — this is non-negotiable. The marketing department had better not continue spending money on campaigns that are not producing results. It's time we invested in proper market research to understand what our customers really want. All department heads had better submit a revised plan by the end of this week. I believe in this team, but it is about time we all showed the kind of commitment that this company was built on.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về những thay đổi cần thiết trong hệ thống giáo dục. Sử dụng 'had better' và 'it's time' để nói về những gì giáo viên, học sinh và nhà trường nên làm khác đi.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: had better, had better not, it's time + to infinitive, it's about/high time + past simple
writing_prompts.example: Education systems around the world are in urgent need of reform. It is high time governments rethought what we expect students to learn and how we measure their success. Schools had better stop placing so much emphasis on memorisation and standardised tests, because this approach is crushing students' creativity and motivation. Teachers had better start using more technology and project-based learning to engage students. It is time to introduce real-life skills such as financial literacy, communication, and problem-solving into the curriculum. It is about time policymakers listened to teachers and students instead of making decisions without consulting them. Students had better also take more responsibility for their own learning outside the classroom. It is high time the education system prioritised mental health and wellbeing alongside academic achievement.

intermediate.title: Would rather & Would sooner
intermediate.content:
📌 Would rather / Would sooner – Sở thích cá nhân (Preference)
Dùng để diễn đạt điều bạn thích làm hơn. Would rather và would sooner có cùng nghĩa, nhưng would rather phổ biến hơn trong giao tiếp hàng ngày:
I'd rather stay home tonight. (= tôi thích ở nhà hơn)
She'd sooner take the train than fly. (= cô ấy thích đi tàu hơn)

✦ Cấu trúc 1 (cùng chủ ngữ): would rather/sooner + bare infinitive (+ than + bare infinitive)
I'd rather walk than take the bus.
He'd sooner cook at home than eat out.

✦ Thể phủ định: would rather/sooner + not + bare infinitive
I'd rather not go out tonight, if you don't mind.
She'd sooner not discuss it right now.

⚠️ SAI LẦM PHỔ BIẾN:
❌ I'd rather to stay home. → ✅ I'd rather stay home.
❌ I'd rather staying home. → ✅ I'd rather stay home.
📌 Would rather + subject + past simple – Muốn người khác làm gì
Cấu trúc 2 (khác chủ ngữ): would rather/sooner + subject + past simple
Dùng khi bạn muốn người KHÁC làm điều gì đó. Mặc dù dùng quá khứ đơn nhưng mang nghĩa hiện tại hoặc tương lai:
I'd rather you stayed home tonight. (= Tôi muốn bạn ở nhà – không phải quá khứ!)
They'd rather their daughter studied medicine.
She'd sooner I called before visiting.

✦ Thể phủ định cấu trúc 2: would rather + subject + didn't/weren't...
I'd rather you didn't come over today.
He'd rather she didn't know about this.

⚠️ SAI LẦM PHỔ BIẾN:
❌ I'd rather not you came over. → ✅ I'd rather you didn't come over.

✦ Câu hỏi: Would + subject + rather + infinitive...?
Would you rather eat in or go out tonight?
📌 Would rather vs Would prefer
Would rather và would prefer đều diễn đạt sở thích, nhưng cấu trúc khác nhau:
Would ratherWould prefer
I'd rather sit inside.I'd prefer to sit inside.
I'd rather not go.I'd prefer not to go.
I'd rather you waited.I'd prefer you to wait. (formal)

For holidays, I'd sooner {{7}} somewhere peaceful and quiet, whereas she'd rather {{8}} an exciting city. If we ever have to choose a film, I'd rather {{9}} a comedy, but she would sooner {{10}} a documentary. Despite our differences, we both agree that we'd rather work together than with anyone else!

Others would rather {{7}} in a stylish area, even if they {{8}} a smaller place. I'd rather {{9}} my decision based on lifestyle needs, not just cost. In the end, people would sooner {{10}} somewhere they truly feel at home.

However, others would sooner {{2}} at a local university to be near their family. Students who go abroad would often rather their families {{3}} them regularly, not just once a year. Some parents would sooner their children {{4}} locally so they can support them easily. When it comes to language, students would rather {{5}} in English if possible, as it opens more career doors. Some students would sooner {{6}} in smaller classes where they can get more attention from lecturers. If they get homesick, they'd rather {{7}} home for a short visit than feel miserable alone. writing_prompts.vi: So sánh sở thích của bạn với sở thích của một người bạn hoặc thành viên gia đình trong ít nhất 5 lĩnh vực khác nhau (ví dụ: ăn uống, du lịch, giải trí, công việc, nhà ở). Sử dụng 'would rather' và 'would sooner'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: would rather + bare infinitive, would rather + subject + past simple, would sooner + infinitive, would rather not
writing_prompts.example: My sister and I have completely different preferences in almost every area of life. When it comes to food, I'd rather cook at home using fresh ingredients, but she would sooner order takeaway every evening. For travel, I would rather explore small, off-the-beaten-track destinations, whereas she'd sooner visit famous cities with lots of tourist attractions. In terms of socialising, I'd rather spend a quiet evening with two or three close friends, but she would rather go to large parties where she meets new people. Regarding entertainment, I'd rather watch a thought-provoking documentary, while she would sooner see a big action film at the cinema. When it comes to work, I'd rather have a flexible schedule and work from home, but my sister would sooner have a structured office routine. Despite our differences, we both agree that we'd rather spend time together as a family than apart.

writing_prompts.vi: Bạn đang có một cuộc tranh luận lịch sự với đồng nghiệp về cách tổ chức một sự kiện công ty. Viết đoạn hội thoại hoặc đoạn văn diễn đạt sở thích của cả hai người, sử dụng 'would rather' và 'would sooner'.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: would rather + bare infinitive, would rather + subject + past simple, would sooner, would rather not
writing_prompts.example: My colleague Mike and I are planning the annual company party, but we have quite different ideas. I'd rather hold the event outdoors in the park, because I think it creates a more relaxed and friendly atmosphere. However, Mike would sooner we booked a formal banquet hall, as he believes it looks more professional. I would rather we spent a larger portion of the budget on entertainment and food than on decoration, but Mike would rather the opposite. I'd rather not invite clients this year, as I feel it puts too much pressure on staff to behave formally. Mike, on the other hand, would rather we included them, as it could strengthen business relationships. In terms of timing, I'd sooner we held it on a Friday evening so people can relax over the weekend. Mike would rather we chose a Saturday afternoon instead. Despite our disagreements, we both agree that we'd rather organise something memorable than repeat the boring event from last year.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một quyết định quan trọng trong cuộc sống của bạn (ví dụ: chọn ngành học, chọn công việc, chọn nơi sống) và lý do tại sao bạn thích lựa chọn này hơn lựa chọn khác.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: would rather + infinitive, would sooner + infinitive + than, would rather + subject + past simple
writing_prompts.example: When I had to choose my university major, I faced a difficult decision between studying business and studying graphic design. My parents would rather I had chosen business, as they believed it offered more job security and a higher salary. However, I would sooner spend my career doing something I am passionate about than earn a high salary in a job I find meaningless. I'd rather work in a creative environment where I can express my ideas than sit in an office managing spreadsheets all day. I also knew I would rather study a subject I genuinely enjoyed, because I believed I would perform much better in my studies. My friends would sooner I chose design too, as they could see how naturally talented I was. In the end, I decided to follow my passion. Looking back, I'd rather have made this choice than taken the safer option, because creativity and fulfilment are more valuable to me than money alone.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một đoạn văn về sở thích của bạn liên quan đến lối sống bền vững và bảo vệ môi trường. Sử dụng 'would rather' để so sánh các lựa chọn thân thiện với môi trường.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: would rather, would sooner, would rather not, would rather + subject + past simple
writing_prompts.example: Living more sustainably has become very important to me in recent years. I would rather cycle to work than drive my car, because cycling produces no pollution and also keeps me fit. I'd sooner buy locally grown vegetables from the market than purchase products that have been flown in from another country. When it comes to energy, I would rather use renewable sources such as solar power than rely on electricity from fossil fuels. I'd rather not buy new clothes every season — instead, I try to buy second-hand or swap items with friends. I would rather governments invested more in public transport than continued building new roads. I'd sooner my community had better recycling facilities than spent money on other things. Of course, change is not always easy, but I'd rather make small positive choices every day than do nothing and watch the environment suffer.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết một bức thư ngắn từ một học sinh gửi cho hiệu trưởng, bày tỏ sở thích về cách cải thiện trường học. Sử dụng 'would rather' và 'would sooner' để diễn đạt các đề xuất lịch sự.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: would rather + infinitive, would rather + subject + past simple, would sooner, would rather not
writing_prompts.example: Dear Principal, I am writing to share some suggestions from the student body about how we feel our school could be improved. Most students would rather have a longer lunch break so that we have time to eat properly and relax before afternoon classes. We would sooner the school canteen offered healthier and more varied food options than continue serving the same limited menu every day. Many students would rather not receive so much homework at weekends, as it leaves us little time to rest and pursue hobbies. We'd rather the school invested in better sports facilities than spent the budget on redecorating the offices. Students would also sooner have the option to study certain subjects online, as this would give us more flexibility. We would rather our teachers gave us more project-based assessments instead of traditional written exams. I hope you will consider these suggestions, as we believe they would make our school a much happier and more productive place for everyone.

intermediate.title: Phrasal verbs 1: Exercises and explanation
intermediate.content:
📌 Phrasal Verbs 1 – 30 Động từ cụm quan trọng ở B1
Phrasal verb là tổ hợp động từ + giới từ/phó từ (particle) tạo ra nghĩa mới khác với nghĩa gốc. Đây là 30 phrasal verbs đầu tiên trong danh sách B1 (Cambridge PET).

🔹 BE OVER (be + over)
If something is over, it has finished.
📝 The concert is over. Let's go home. / Is the match over yet?

🔹 BREAK DOWN (break + down)
If a car or machine breaks down, it stops working.
📝 Our car broke down on the motorway. / The printer has broken down again.

🔹 BREAK IN / BREAK INTO (break + in/into)
If someone breaks in or breaks into a building, they enter it by force to steal.
📝 Someone broke in and stole the TV. / Burglars broke into the shop last night.

🔹 BREAK UP (break + up)
When two people break up, they end their relationship.
📝 Tom and Lucy broke up after two years together. / Why did they break up?

🔹 BRING UP (bring + up)
When someone brings up a child, they raise them. Also, to mention a topic.
📝 She was brought up by her grandparents. / Don't bring up politics at dinner.

🔹 CALL FOR (call + for)
If you call for someone, you go to the place where they are so that you can both go somewhere together.
📝 I'll call for you at seven o'clock. / He called for his friend before the match.

🔹 CARRY ON (carry + on)
If you carry on doing something, you continue doing it.
📝 Carry on with your work – I'll be back soon. / She carried on talking despite the noise.

🔹 CARRY OUT (carry + out)
If you carry out a task or instruction, you do it or act according to the plan in order to complete it.
📝 The scientists carried out a series of experiments. / Please carry out the instructions carefully.

🔹 CHECK OUT (check + out)
When you check out of a hotel or guest house, you leave the place after paying and returning your room key.
📝 We need to check out by eleven o'clock. / She checked out early and caught the morning train.

🔹 COME ON (come + on)
Used to tell someone to hurry or to encourage them.
📝 Come on! We'll be late! / Come on – you can do it!

🔹 CROSS OUT (cross + out)
If you cross out words, you draw a line through them because they are wrong.
📝 Cross out the wrong answer and write the correct one. / She crossed out her mistake.

🔹 CUT UP (cut + up)
If you cut something up, you cut it into several pieces.
📝 Cut up the vegetables before adding them to the pan. / He cut up the paper into small squares.

🔹 DEAL WITH (deal + with)
When you deal with something, you give it attention and often solve a problem.
📝 We need to deal with this complaint immediately. / How did you deal with the situation?

🔹 DEPEND ON (depend + on)
If something depends on something else, it is affected or decided by it.
📝 The plan depends on the weather. / She depends on her family for support.

🔹 END UP (end + up)
If you end up somewhere, you unexpectedly find yourself there after a series of events.
📝 We got lost and ended up in the wrong city. / She ended up working for the company she hated.

🔹 FILL IN (fill + in)
If you fill in a form, you write the necessary information in its spaces.
📝 Please fill in this application form. / Fill in all the blanks carefully.

🔹 FILL UP (fill + up)
If you fill up a container or tank, it becomes full.
📝 Fill up the car with petrol before the long journey. / She filled up her water bottle.

🔹 FIND OUT (find + out)
If you find something out, you learn or discover something you didn't know.
📝 I found out that the shop was closed. / How did you find out about the party?

🔹 GET ALONG (get + along)
If you get along with someone, you have a friendly relationship.
📝 Tom and I get along really well. / Do you get along with your colleagues?

🔹 GET BACK (get + back)
If you get back to a place, you return to it.
📝 When did you get back from holiday? / I'll get back to you with an answer tomorrow.

🔹 GET DOWN (get + down)
If you get down, you lower your body. Also, to write something down.
📝 Get down from the table! / Get down the address before you forget it.

🔹 GET IN (get + in)
If you get in, you enter a place, especially when it's difficult.
📝 He got in through the window. / What time did you get in last night?

🔹 GET OFF (get + off)
When you get off a bus, train or vehicle, you leave it.
📝 Get off at the next stop. / She got off the plane feeling exhausted.

🔹 GET ON (get + on)
If you get on with something, you start or continue doing it. Also, to manage well.
📝 Get on with your homework! / How are you getting on with the new project?

🔹 GET RID OF (get + rid + of)
If you get rid of something, you remove it so you no longer have to deal with it.
📝 I want to get rid of all this old furniture. / How can I get rid of this headache?

🔹 GIVE BACK (give + back)
If you give something back, you return it to the person who gave it to you.
📝 Please give me back my pen. / She gave back the money she had borrowed.

🔹 GIVE IN (give + in)
If you give in, you surrender or stop arguing. Also, to hand in work.
📝 Don't give in to pressure – stand your ground. / Have you given in your assignment yet?

🔹 GIVE OUT (give + out)
If you give out something, you distribute it to a group of people.
📝 The teacher gave out the exam papers. / They gave out free samples at the entrance.

🔹 GIVE UP (give + up)
If you give up something, you stop doing it or taking it.
📝 I'm going to give up smoking. / She gave up her job to travel the world.

🔹 GIVE WAY (give + way)
If you give way to a vehicle, you allow it to go past before you move.
📝 Give way to traffic coming from the right. / She gave way and let the ambulance pass.

🔹 HAND IN (hand + in)
If you hand in work or an object, you give it to a person in authority.
📝 Please hand in your essays by Friday. / He handed in his resignation letter.

intermediate.title: Phrasal verbs 2: Exercises and explanation
intermediate.content:
📌 Phrasal Verbs 2 – Phrasal Verbs 31–60
Tiếp tục danh sách B1 Phrasal Verbs từ Cambridge PET. Đây là 30 phrasal verbs tiếp theo (số 31–60).

🔹 GO FOR (go + for)
If you go for something, you try to have or achieve it.
📝 Go for it – you have nothing to lose! / She went for the top prize.

🔹 GO OFF (go + off)
If an alarm goes off, it starts making noise. If food goes off, it becomes bad.
📝 The alarm went off at six a.m. / The milk has gone off.

🔹 GO ON (go + on)
If something goes on, it continues to happen or exist.
📝 What's going on? / The storm went on for three days.

🔹 GO OUT (go + out)
If you go out, you leave your home to do something fun. Also, if you go out with someone, you have a romantic relationship.
📝 We're going out for dinner tonight. / She's been going out with him for two years.

🔹 GO WITH (go + with)
If one thing goes with another, they look or taste good together.
📝 This wine goes well with fish. / Does this jacket go with these trousers?

🔹 GO TOGETHER (go + together)
If two things go together, they look or work well together.
📝 These colours go together beautifully. / Pizza and cola go together perfectly.

🔹 GROW UP (grow + up)
When someone grows up, they change from a child into an adult.
📝 She grew up in a small town near the sea. / He wants to be a doctor when he grows up.

🔹 HAND IN (hand + in)
If you hand in work or something you've found, you give it to a teacher, police, etc.
📝 Please hand in your homework by Friday. / She handed in her ID to the security guard.

🔹 HAND OUT (hand + out)
If you hand things out, you give one to each person in a group.
📝 The teacher handed out the exam papers. / Volunteers handed out food to the homeless.

🔹 HANG OUT (hang + out)
If you hang out with someone, you spend time with them in a relaxed way.
📝 We usually hang out at the park after school. / Where do teenagers hang out in this city?

🔹 HANG UP (hang + up)
If you hang up, you end a phone call.
📝 She hung up before I could explain. / Don't hang up – I need to tell you something!

🔹 HOLD UP (hold + up)
To hold up someone or something means to delay them.
📝 Sorry I'm late – I was held up in traffic. / The accident held up traffic for two hours.

🔹 KEEP IN (keep + in)
If you keep someone in a place, you make them stay there.
📝 The doctor kept him in hospital for observation. / Her parents kept her in after dark.

🔹 KEEP ON (keep + on)
If you keep on doing something, you continue doing it.
📝 Keep on practising and you'll improve. / He kept on talking even when no one was listening.

🔹 KEEP UP (keep + up)
If you keep up something good, you continue to do it well.
📝 Keep up the good work! / She found it hard to keep up with the rest of the class.

🔹 KNOCK DOWN (knock + down)
If a person is knocked down by a vehicle, they are hit by it and fall to the ground. Also, to hit someone so that they fall.
📝 The cyclist was knocked down by a bus near the school. / He knocked down his opponent in the first round.

🔹 LIE DOWN (lie + down)
When you lie down, you move into a horizontal position, usually in order to rest or sleep.
📝 I need to lie down for a few minutes – I feel dizzy. / She lay down on the sofa after work.

🔹 LOOK AFTER (look + after)
If you look after someone or something, you take care of them.
📝 Can you look after my cat while I'm away? / He looks after elderly patients at the hospital.

🔹 LOOK FOR (look + for)
If you look for something, you try to find it.
📝 I'm looking for my phone – have you seen it? / She's looking for a new job.

🔹 LOOK FORWARD TO (look + forward + to)
If you look forward to something, you feel excited because it will happen soon.
📝 I'm looking forward to the holidays! / She's looking forward to meeting you.

🔹 LOOK OUT (look + out)
If you say 'Look out!', you warn someone they are in danger.
📝 Look out! There's a car coming! / Look out for icy patches on the road.

🔹 LOOK UP (look + up)
If you look up information, you try to find it in a book or on a computer.
📝 I'll look up the train times online. / She looked up the word in the dictionary.

🔹 PASS ON (pass + on)
If you pass something on, you give it to someone else after receiving it yourself.
📝 Could you pass on this message to your manager? / The tradition was passed on from generation to generation.

🔹 PICK UP (pick + up)
When you pick up someone, you collect them from a place, usually in a car.
📝 I'll pick you up from the airport. / Can you pick up some milk on the way home?

🔹 PUT AWAY (put + away)
If you put something away, you put it back in the place where it is normally kept.
📝 Please put your books away and listen. / She put away her clothes neatly.

🔹 PUT DOWN (put + down)
If you put down something, especially your name, on a list or piece of paper, you write it down.
📝 Put down your name and phone number here. / She put down the address before she forgot it.

🔹 PUT OFF (put + off)
If you put something off, you delay it.
📝 Stop putting off your homework! / The meeting was put off until Thursday.

🔹 PUT ON (put + on)
If you put on clothes, you dress yourself. Also, to start a device. Also, if you put on weight, you become fatter.
📝 Put on your coat – it's cold outside. / She put on the television and sat down.

writing_prompts.example: The person I admire most is my grandmother, who grew up in rural Vietnam in the 1950s. Life was extremely hard, and there were many days when she felt completely knocked down by exhaustion and hardship. Despite all of this, she never gave up on her dreams. She kept on studying whenever she could, reading by candlelight after long days of farm work. She looked after her younger siblings when her parents were working. When she was young, she would look forward to market days as a rare chance to explore and meet new people. She went for every opportunity she was given. Over time, she passed on her values of hard work and determination to her children and grandchildren.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết về một chuyến đi bạn đã trải qua. Mô tả hành trình từ đầu đến cuối. Sử dụng phrasal verbs từ PV2.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: phrasal verbs 2: pick up, hang out, look for, look forward to, put off, go off, look out, go out, go with
writing_prompts.example: Last spring, three friends and I decided to take a spontaneous road trip to the central highlands. We had been looking forward to it for weeks. I picked up my friends from their homes one by one. The journey was long, but we hung out in the car chatting and sharing snacks. At one point, we were held up by roadworks for nearly an hour. Then our GPS went off and we had to look for the right road using a paper map. We had initially put off visiting one particular waterfall because it seemed too far, but we decided to go for it. Look out — the path was steep and slippery! That evening, we went out for dinner at a local restaurant. The food really went with the setting — simple, fresh and delicious.

writing_prompts.vi: Viết đoạn văn mô tả quy trình chuẩn bị cho một sự kiện quan trọng. Sử dụng phrasal verbs từ PV2.
writing_prompts.grammar_focus: phrasal verbs 2: hand out, look after, look for, pick up, put on, put away, pass on, look forward to, put down, knock down
writing_prompts.example: Organising our school's annual talent show took several weeks. The student committee handed out sign-up sheets to every class. We looked for suitable costumes and props from local hire shops. On the day of the show, student helpers put on their matching volunteer T-shirts. They looked after the younger children backstage, making sure everyone was calm. One team member passed on the running order to each performer and put down extra notes on a clipboard for reference. Another team put away chairs and cleared the stage between acts. We kept on working until everything was ready. I picked up the final props from the storage room just ten minutes before the curtain opened. When the audience applauded, every volunteer agreed it had all been worth it. We were already looking forward to making it even better next year.

intermediate.title: Phrasal verbs 3: Exercises and explanation
intermediate.content:
📌 Phrasal Verbs 3 – Phrasal Verbs 61–90
Đây là các phrasal verbs cuối trong bộ B1 (Cambridge PET). Làm chủ tất cả giúp bạn sẵn sàng cho kỳ thi Cambridge B1 Preliminary.

🔹 PUT OUT (put + out)
If you put out a fire, cigarette, etc., you make it stop burning.
📝 The firefighters put out the blaze quickly. / Please put out your cigarette before entering.

🔹 PUT THROUGH (put + through)
If you put through a call or the person calling, you connect them with the person they want to speak to.
📝 Could you put me through to the manager, please? / She put the call through immediately.

🔹 PUT UP (put + up)
If you put up the price of something, you increase its price. Also, to display or build something.
📝 They put up the prices last month. / She put up posters around the school.

🔹 RING BACK (ring + back)
If you ring someone back, you return a previous call they made to you.
📝 I'll ring you back after the meeting. / He rang back as soon as he saw the missed call.

🔹 RING UP (ring + up)
If you ring up someone, you phone them.
📝 Ring me up when you get home safely. / She rang up the hotel to confirm the booking.

🔹 RUN OUT (run + out)
If you run out of something, you have no more of it because you've used it all.
📝 We've run out of milk – can you buy some? / He ran out of time during the exam.

🔹 SET OFF (set + off)
When you set off, you start a journey. Also, to cause something to start.
📝 We set off at dawn to avoid traffic. / The smoke alarm set off a panic.

🔹 SET OUT (set + out)
If you set out to do or achieve something, you start an activity with a particular aim.
📝 She set out to prove everyone wrong. / He set out to climb the tallest peak.

🔹 SET UP (set + up)
If you set up a business, organisation, etc., you start it. Also, to make preparations for something.
📝 They set up a new business last year. / Can you set up the projector before the meeting?

🔹 SHOW UP (show + up)
If someone shows up, they arrive somewhere to join other people.
📝 He didn't show up to the party. / I was relieved when she finally showed up.

🔹 SIT DOWN (sit + down)
The verbs sit down and sit mean the same.
📝 Please sit down and make yourself comfortable. / She sat down and opened her book.

🔹 SPLIT UP (split + up)
If two people split up, or if someone splits up with someone else, they end their relationship.
📝 They split up after three years together. / She split up with her boyfriend last month.

🔹 STAY BEHIND (stay + behind)
If you stay behind, you remain in a place after everyone else has left.
📝 She stayed behind after class to ask the teacher a question. / Can you stay behind for a moment?

🔹 TAKE OFF (take + off)
If you take off a piece of clothing, you remove it. Also, when an aeroplane takes off, it leaves the ground and starts flying.
📝 The plane took off on time. / He took off his shoes at the door.

🔹 TAKE UP (take + up)
If you take up an activity or a subject, you start doing it as a job, habit or interest. Also, to fill space or time.
📝 She took up yoga last year and loves it. / The sofa takes up too much space.

🔹 THROW AWAY (throw + away)
If you throw away something, you get rid of it, for example, by putting it in a trash container.
📝 I threw away all my old magazines. / Don't throw away the receipt – you might need it.

🔹 TIDY UP (tidy + up)
When you tidy up a place, you put things back in their right places so that everything is neat.
📝 Please tidy up your room before dinner. / We spent an hour tidying up after the party.

🔹 TRY ON (try + on)
If you try on a piece of clothing, you put it on to see if it fits and you like it.
📝 Can I try on this dress in the changing room? / She tried on three pairs of shoes before deciding.

🔹 TURN DOWN (turn + down)
If you turn down the level or volume of something, you decrease it. Also, to refuse an offer or request.
📝 Could you turn down the music, please? / She turned down the job offer.

🔹 TURN INTO (turn + into)
If someone or something turns into someone or something else, they change and become this other thing/person.
📝 The caterpillar turned into a butterfly. / What started as a small argument turned into a serious dispute.

🔹 TURN OFF (turn + off)
When you turn off a piece of equipment, you make it stop working, usually by pressing a button.
📝 Turn off the television – it's bedtime. / She turned off the engine.

🔹 TURN ON (turn + on)
When you turn on a piece of equipment, you make it start working, usually by pressing a button.
📝 Can you turn on the heating? It's freezing! / He turned on his laptop to start working.

🔹 TURN UP (turn + up)
If you turn up the level or volume of something, you increase it. Also, if someone turns up, they arrive.
📝 He turned up two hours late without any explanation. / Could you turn up the volume a bit?

🔹 WAKE UP (wake + up)
When you wake up or when someone or something wakes you up, you become conscious after sleeping.
📝 I wake up at seven every morning. / The noise woke me up in the middle of the night.

🔹 WASH UP (wash + up)
When you wash up, you clean part of your body with soap and water, especially your hands and face. Also, if you wash up, you wash the things used for cooking and eating.
📝 Go and wash up before dinner. / She washed up all the plates and glasses after the party.

🔹 WEAR OUT (wear + out)
If something wears out or if you wear something out, it is used so much that it can no longer be used.
📝 My running shoes have completely worn out. / Looking after three young children wore her out.

🔹 WORK OUT (work + out)
When you work out, you do physical exercises to be fit and strong.
📝 She works out at the gym three times a week. / He works out every morning before breakfast.

🔹 WRAP UP (wrap + up)
If you wrap something up, you cover it by putting something like paper or cloth around it.
📝 She wrapped up the birthday present neatly. / Make sure you wrap up the fragile items carefully.

🔹 WRITE DOWN (write + down)
When you write something down, you write it on a piece of paper using a pen or pencil.
📝 Write down the address before you forget it. / She wrote down everything the teacher said.

🔹 SHOW OFF (show + off)
If you show off, you try to impress others by behaving in a way to attract attention.
📝 He was showing off his new car to the neighbours. / She's always showing off at school.

🔹 SORT OUT (sort + out)
If you sort out a problem or situation, you deal with it successfully.
📝 We need to sort out this misunderstanding quickly. / She sorted out all the paperwork.

🔹 STAND UP (stand + up)
If someone stands you up, they fail to meet you at an arranged time.
📝 He stood me up for our date. / Don't stand up your friends – it's very rude.

🔹 SWITCH ON (switch + on)
If you switch something on, you make it start working using a switch.
📝 Switch on the lights – it's dark in here. / She switched on the coffee machine.

🔹 SWITCH OFF (switch + off)
If you switch something off, you stop it working using a switch.
📝 Don't forget to switch off the lights when you leave. / He switched off his phone during the film.

🔹 TAKE AFTER (take + after)
If you take after a family member, you look or behave like them.
📝 She takes after her mother – same smile and personality. / He takes after his father in many ways.

🔹 TAKE AWAY (take + away)
If you take away something, you remove it. Also, takeaway food.
📝 Please take away these old boxes. / We ordered takeaway because we were too tired to cook.

🔹 TAKE BACK (take + back)
If you take something back, you return it to the shop or person you got it from.
📝 This coat doesn't fit – I need to take it back. / She took back everything she had said.

🔹 TAKE ON (take + on)
If you take on work or responsibility, you accept it.
📝 She took on too much work and became stressed. / The company took on twenty new employees.

🔹 TAKE OVER (take + over)
If you take over, you gain control of or responsibility for something.
📝 A new manager took over the department. / He took over the family business at 30.

🔹 TURN OUT (turn + out)
If something turns out in a particular way, that is the result or end.
📝 The project turned out to be a great success. / It turned out that she was right all along.
